sections.paragraph:
Arab. تاز for تيز (pr. to cut off, cut away), intrans. to die (to be cut away), تَيَّازُ dwarf (pr. cut off, shortened), whence the Heb.

Hiphil הֵתֵז (as if from תָּזַז) in pause הֵהַז to cut off the tendrils of a vine, Isa 18:5. Talmud. הֵתִיז and הִתִּיז is to cut off the head.
